programs.title: Man v Food
programs.desc: Adam Richman travels around the United States to face the greatest culinary challenges.It covers the route from Boston to Seattle.He also goes to any other place where he can overeat.

programs.title: Taste the world with Pascal
programs.desc:
Pascal Brodnicki likes to explore the world through his sense of taste.He loves to prepare various dishes at street stalls, in African villages or on the beach. However, during his culinary journeys it was not always easy for him.Sometimes elephants stole his ingredients.Of course, Pascal couldn't deny himself the pleasure of tasting local delicacies.In Delhi, he stopped for a long time at a paratha stand, in a Zanzibar village he ate rice with coconut, and in Namibia he tried a zebra steak."Taste the world with Pascal" is not only about cooking.The host visited an elephant orphanage in Sri Lanka, fed cheetahs in Africa and climbed a coconut tree.In Bombay he became a teacher and taught Polish to children, and on the coast of Zanzibar he hunted octopus.Everywhere his passion and positive energy attracted crowds of onlookers
The first stop on Pascal's culinary journey is Sri Lanka.The cook visits the Pinawela center - an elephant orphanage.These huge mammals can eat up to 300 kg of food a day and they love carrots.Pascal will prepare a meal for these unusual gourmets.After a busy day, the chef decides to thank his newfound friends and invites them for dinner on the shores of the Indian Ocean.He chooses a quick recipe for chicken curry with the addition of cashew nuts, which are very popular on the island.

programs.title: Taste the world with Pascal
programs.desc:
Pascal Brodnicki likes to explore the world through his sense of taste.He loves to prepare various dishes at street stalls, in African villages or on the beach. However, during his culinary journeys it was not always easy for him.Sometimes elephants stole his ingredients.Of course, Pascal couldn't deny himself the pleasure of tasting local delicacies.In Delhi, he stopped for a long time at a paratha stand, in a Zanzibar village he ate rice with coconut, and in Namibia he tried a zebra steak."Taste the world with Pascal" is not only about cooking.The host visited an elephant orphanage in Sri Lanka, fed cheetahs in Africa and climbed a coconut tree.In Bombay he became a teacher and taught Polish to children, and on the coast of Zanzibar he hunted octopus.Everywhere his passion and positive energy attracted crowds of onlookers
Pascal continues to explore the flavors of Sri Lanka - a country that is a leader in tea production.The cook decides to learn the secrets of working on the plantation.The appearance of an almost two-meter tall man will cause quite a sensation among the women working there.Pascal will also visit Negombo.The city on the west coast of the island is known mainly for its old market where you can buy various types of fish - from mackerel, through shark, to "flying fish".All fresh, straight from the sea.While exploring the coast, Pascal meets local fishermen.He offers them help, hoping that he will learn the secrets of preparing fish.However, it quickly turns out that he will have to prove himself as a trade fair seller.

programs.title: Taste the world with Pascal
programs.desc:
Pascal Brodnicki likes to explore the world through his sense of taste.He loves to prepare various dishes at street stalls, in African villages or on the beach. However, during his culinary journeys it was not always easy for him.Sometimes elephants stole his ingredients.Of course, Pascal couldn't deny himself the pleasure of tasting local delicacies.In Delhi, he stopped for a long time at a paratha stand, in a Zanzibar village he ate rice with coconut, and in Namibia he tried a zebra steak."Taste the world with Pascal" is not only about cooking.The host visited an elephant orphanage in Sri Lanka, fed cheetahs in Africa and climbed a coconut tree.In Bombay he became a teacher and taught Polish to children, and on the coast of Zanzibar he hunted octopus.Everywhere his passion and positive energy attracted crowds of onlookers
This time Pascal went to Namibia, famous for its cow breeding and delicious beef.The chef grills his favorite steaks here and, as usual, he will do it in an unusual place.This time on the farm where they are kept.tame cheetahs.After meeting the big cats, Pascal sets off north of the country, to Opuwo, where he goes to a supermarket.Here he meets half-naked women from the Himba tribe who are shopping.He also visits the market where he buys a gift for the Herrero tribe - a goat.His gesture wins great acclaim and Pascal is welcomed into the village with an amazing dance.

programs.title: Taste the world with Pascal
programs.desc:
Pascal Brodnicki likes to explore the world through his sense of taste.He loves to prepare various dishes at street stalls, in African villages or on the beach. However, during his culinary journeys it was not always easy for him.Sometimes elephants stole his ingredients.Of course, Pascal couldn't deny himself the pleasure of tasting local delicacies.In Delhi, he stopped for a long time at a paratha stand, in a Zanzibar village he ate rice with coconut, and in Namibia he tried a zebra steak."Taste the world with Pascal" is not only about cooking.The host visited an elephant orphanage in Sri Lanka, fed cheetahs in Africa and climbed a coconut tree.In Bombay he became a teacher and taught Polish to children, and on the coast of Zanzibar he hunted octopus.Everywhere his passion and positive energy attracted crowds of onlookers
The next stop on the "Taste the World with Pascal" map is India.Pascal will visit Delhi, where he will taste parathas, traditional wheat pancakes, served with green peas, carrots, bananas or cheese at street stalls.At one of the stalls, Pascal will serve passers-by chicken "Indian style" according to his own recipe.The leader will also visit temples where volunteers prepare free meals for pilgrims.In such kitchens, people cook for up to a thousand people.The cook had never cooked on such a scale.Later, Pascal will go to Bombay, where he will look for the perfect fish for his recipe at the fish market.And then he will prepare it in curry sauce, with peppers and coconut milk.A saleswoman at the local market lends him the kitchen.

programs.title: Indonesia's Best Ever Eats
programs.desc: A culinary journey to the largest island country in the world.Gourmets will discover the best dishes from five regions of Indonesia, including the area around Lake Toba in Sumatra.
